per_finding.title: Cross-contract reentrancy allows YIELD_TOKEN theft for the GenericERC4626 WithdrawalReq
mech_hits: [ARITHMETIC] AbstractYieldStrategy::_mintSharesGivenAssets — sharesMinted = (yieldTokensMinted * effectiveSupply()) / (initialYieldTokenBalance - feesAccrued() + VIRTUAL_YIELD_TOKENS) — if initialYieldTokenBalance < feesAccrued(), subtraction underflows in unchecked context

[TOKEN ACCOUNTING] AbstractWithdrawRequestManager::initiateWithdraw — ERC20(YIELD_TOKEN).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), yieldTokenAmount) — if YIELD_TOKEN is rebasing, yieldTokenAmount on contract could change before _initiateWithdrawImpl, requestId tied to wrong amount
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [REENTRANCY] AbstractRewardManager::_claimVaultRewards — _executeClaim() is virtual and calls external rewardPool before updating lastClaimTimestamp — Reentrancy could allow double-claim if _executeCl

per_finding.title: Attacker can drain the entire suppliers on
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC] TimelockUpgradeableProxy::UPGRADE_DELAY — 7 days gives attacker time to extract value before malicious upgrade is stopped — governance has no emergency cancel, must wait 7

per_finding.title: DineroWithdrawRequestManager vulnerable to token overwithdrawal via batch ID overlap
mech_hits: [ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION] DineroWithdrawRequestManager::_initiateWithdrawImpl — nonce is s_batchNonce++ but no check if nonce is already at max — if nonce == type(uint16).max, increment wraps to zero, requestId collision

[ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION] DineroWithdrawRequestManager::_decodeBatchIds — requestId is encoded with bit shifts but no validation on decode — if requestId is malformed, initialBatchId or finalBatchId could be garbage
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [TOKEN ACCOUNTING] AbstractSingleSidedLP::_executeDepositTrades — assetRemaining underflow check relies on revert, no explicit check — Could silently succeed with corrupted state if underflow happens

per_finding.title: When users borrow directly from Morpho
mech_hits: [ACCESS CONTROL & INIT] MorphoLendingRouter::initializeMarket — can only be called once per vault, no checks on irm/lltv sanity — malicious or incorrect parameters could brick the market permanently

[ORACLE / PRICE FEED] MorphoLendingRouter::healthFactor — calls MORPHO.accrueInterest and IYieldStrategy(vault).price(borrower) in same tx — if price() has side effects or depends on accrued state, ordering matters and could be exploited
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY] MorphoLendingRouter::onMorphoLiquidate — transfers tokens then approves MORPHO — if liquidator is malicious contract, could reenter during safeTransferFrom and exploit ap

per_finding.title: migrateRewardPool Fails Due to Incompatible Storage Design in CurveConvexLib
mech_hits: [ARITHMETIC] CurveConvexLib::_exitPool — exitBalances[0] = TokenUtils.tokenBalance(TOKEN_1) - exitBalances[0] could underflow — If tokens were somehow transferred in before the call, subtraction underflows
[TOKEN ACCOUNTING] CurveConvexLib::joinPoolAndStake — WETH.withdraw called without checking success — Failed unwrap could leave contract in inconsistent state
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [EXTERNAL CALLS] AbstractStakingStrategy::_mintYieldTokens — withdrawRequestManager.stakeTokens assumes success — Could fail silently leaving assets unstaked

per_finding.title: DoS might happen to DineroWithdrawReq
mech_hits: [ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION] DineroWithdrawRequestManager::_initiateWithdrawImpl — nonce is s_batchNonce++ but no check if nonce is already at max — if nonce == type(uint16).max, increment wraps to zero, requestId collision

[ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION] DineroWithdrawRequestManager::_decodeBatchIds — requestId is encoded with bit shifts but no validation on decode — if requestId is malformed, initialBatchId or finalBatchId could be garbage
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [ARITHMETIC] IYieldStrategy::convertToAssets — no rounding direction specified — asymmetric rounding could systematically favor users over protocol or vice versa

per_finding.title: RewardManagerMixin.claimAccountRewar
mech_hits: [ACCESS CONTROL] RewardManagerMixin::claimAccountRewards — fallback() does not restrict which RewardManager functions can be called — Could allow unauthorized calls to privileged functions through delegatecall

[TOKEN ACCOUNTING] RewardManagerMixin::_burnShares — wasEscrowed determined before burn but shares may not actually be in valid escrow state — Could apply wrong reward logic if escrow state is invalid
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [EXTERNAL CALLS] PendlePTLib::swapExactTokenForPt — PENDLE_ROUTER.swapExactTokenForPt returns values not validated — Could return zero or unexpected amounts

mech_hits: [ORACLE / PRICE FEED] PendlePTOracle::_getPTRate — uses twapDuration but no check if TWAP window is manipulable — attacker could manipulate Pendle pool price within the TWAP window to extract value

per_finding.title: Rounding discrepancy between
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [ARITHMETIC] IYieldStrategy::convertToAssets — no rounding direction specified — asymmetric rounding could systematically favor users over protocol or vice versa

per_finding.title: Emission rewards will keep accruing even
mech_hits: [ARITHMETIC] AbstractRewardManager::_getAccumulatedRewardViaEmissionRate — (timeSinceLastAccumulation * DEFAULT_PRECISION * state.emissionRatePerYear) could overflow — Could cause reward calculation to revert or wrap

[ECONOMIC] AbstractRewardManager::_accumulateSecondaryRewardViaEmissionRate — endTime checked but if block.timestamp == endTime, time is set to endTime and last accumulation could be skipped — Off-by-one could lose final block's rewards
per_finding.thematic_best_lead: [ACCESS CONTROL] AbstractRewardManager::updateRewardToken — No validation that endTime > lastAccumulatedTime when setting emission rate — Could result in immediate expiry or underflow in accumulation
